executive_summary.overview: Anglepoint conducted a Quest software asset management engagement for Regeneron, delivering a Risk & Opportunity Assessment Report (ROAR) in June 2025. The engagement identified compliance risks and cost avoidance opportunities across Quest products including Change Auditor, Recovery Manager for AD, and Toad, with a renewal date approaching September 15th, 2025.
key_accomplishments: Mitigated $141K of initial compliance risk through additional license purchases for Change Auditor for AD and Recovery Manager for AD
Identified $24K in cost avoidance opportunities through proactive compliance and financial controls
Completed comprehensive Effective License Position (ELP) analysis across Quest product portfolio with $248K annual spend

key_metrics.label: Total Identified Risk
key_metrics.context: Quantified financial exposure due to non-compliance with Quest licensing terms

key_metrics.label: Cost Avoidance Identified
key_metrics.context: Unbudgeted costs preventable through proactive compliance measures

key_metrics.label: Risk Mitigated
key_metrics.context: Compliance risk resolved through additional license purchases

recommendations: Obtain Toad license key information by requesting screenshots from end users to determine the edition of Toad deployed and improve Consumption Analysis accuracy
Optimize Toad installations by removing all products and components not in use from the environment
Prioritize software inventory based on what is installed, licensed, and needed to identify remediation, optimization, and cancellation opportunities
primary_risks: Remaining unmitigated compliance risk exists after $141K remediation, requiring continued monitoring ahead of September 2025 renewal
Incomplete Toad deployment data limits accuracy of consumption analysis and may mask additional compliance exposure
No contracts were provided for review, limiting visibility into contractual terms and potential compliance obligations
market_risks: Quest renewal approaching September 15th, 2025 requires timely resolution of outstanding compliance and optimization actions
additional_insights: Year-over-Year comparison shows sustained cost avoidance accomplishments through proactive license management
Demand forecasting data collection enables 3-18 month usage projections for strategic planning
Anglepoint team includes Director of Client Success, Lead Consultant for Specialty & SaaS, and Senior Manager for Specialty & SaaS
next_steps: Procure additional licenses for Change Auditor for AD and Recovery Manager for AD - COMPLETED
Request Toad license key screenshots from end users to determine deployed editions
Remove unused Toad products and components from environment to optimize license consumption
Prepare for Quest renewal on September 15th, 2025 with optimized license position

executive_summary.overview: Encova Insurance IBM Risk & Opportunity Assessment Report for January 2026, covering SAM managed services for 9 distributed IBM products across 262 servers and 50 containers. Anglepoint's IBM License Management team conducted comprehensive license position analysis through their CVA (Contract Value Assessment) program.
key_accomplishments: Identified and accomplished $222K in cost avoidance through proactive compliance and financial controls
Identified $507K in total cost optimization opportunities over a 1-year period
Accomplished $282K in verified cost optimization through software and contract adjustments
Completed comprehensive analysis of 262 servers and 50 containers running IBM software
Analyzed 1 ILMT instance for license compliance monitoring

key_metrics.label: Total Identified Risk
key_metrics.context: Quantified financial exposure due to non-compliance with IBM licensing or contractual terms

key_metrics.label: Identified Cost Avoidance
key_metrics.context: Potential unbudgeted costs prevented through proactive compliance controls

key_metrics.label: Accomplished Cost Avoidance
key_metrics.context: 100% of identified cost avoidance successfully realized

key_metrics.label: Identified Cost Optimization
key_metrics.context: Opportunities to reduce software expenses through license optimization and contract negotiations

key_metrics.label: Accomplished Cost Optimization
key_metrics.context: Verified cost reductions achieved through optimization efforts (56% of identified)

key_metrics.label: Servers Analyzed
key_metrics.context: Distributed servers running IBM software within engagement scope

recommendations: Continue IBM CVA Services to maintain ongoing IBM software license management through the CVA program
Execute Annual S&S Renewal considering potential reduction opportunities identified in the analysis
Expand Publisher Scope to include Oracle, Microsoft, VMware, and other major publishers for comprehensive SAM coverage
primary_risks: Identified compliance risk of $222K due to non-compliance with IBM software licensing or contractual terms requiring corrective action
Unrealized cost optimization of $225K ($507K identified minus $282K accomplished) represents potential savings not yet captured
market_risks: IBM licensing complexity across distributed and containerized environments requires continuous monitoring and ILMT management
Annual S&S renewal presents opportunity for cost reduction but requires strategic preparation
additional_insights: Engagement scope covers 9 distributed IBM products requiring active license management
ILMT instance deployment enables sub-capacity licensing compliance tracking
Anglepoint recognized as Gartner Leader in SAM Managed Services for 6 consecutive years
Full engagement process includes Contract Discovery, Deployment Discovery, EEP, Consumption Position, ELP, and ROAR deliverables
next_steps: Continue IBM CVA Services for ongoing license management and compliance monitoring
Execute Annual S&S Renewal with focus on identified reduction opportunities
Expand publisher scope to Oracle, Microsoft, VMware for comprehensive multi-publisher SAM coverage
Complete Quarterly CVA Submission as part of managed service cadence

executive_summary.overview: UPS engaged Anglepoint for comprehensive IBM license management covering up to 250 IBM products across 70,000 servers, 270,000 workstations, 5 mainframes, and 10,000 Red Hat servers. The engagement identified $8.9M in total optimization opportunities through risk mitigation and license right-sizing across both sub-capacity and non-ILMT products.
key_accomplishments: Achieved full $1.08M cost avoidance by resolving compliance risks across 8 IBM products including InfoSphere DataStage, WebSphere Application Server, and DB2 editions
Identified $3.59M in potential cost savings from sub-capacity products with 23 products having more licenses than deployed
Identified $2.56M in potential cost savings from non-ILMT products with 43 products over-licensed
Reduced IBM ODM Server deployment from 23,400 to 7,420 licenses representing $858K in deployment trend savings
Completed Effective License Position analysis across 115 total IBM distributed products

key_metrics.label: Total Optimization Identified
key_metrics.context: Combined cost avoidance and savings opportunities over 1-year period

key_metrics.label: Sub-Capacity Products Over-Licensed
key_metrics.context: Products with more licenses than current deployment requires

key_metrics.label: Non-ILMT Products Over-Licensed
key_metrics.context: Products with excess licenses identified for potential renewal reduction

key_metrics.label: Zero-Deployment Products (Sub-Capacity)
key_metrics.context: Products carrying active S&S with no deployments detected

key_metrics.label: Zero-Deployment Products (Non-ILMT)
key_metrics.context: Non-ILMT products with active maintenance but no usage

recommendations: Remove 23 IBM sub-capacity products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal if no growth planned - $2.34M potential savings
Validate and remove 17 sub-capacity products with 0% deployment from renewal - $2.91M potential savings
Remove 43 non-ILMT products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal - $1.82M potential savings
Confirm 32 non-ILMT products with 0% deployment are not in use and remove from renewal - $743K potential savings
Schedule WatsonX implementation with product stakeholders as next phase initiative
primary_risks: IBM DB2 Enterprise Server Edition deployment increased from 43,280 to 46,800 representing $107K in additional licensing exposure
IBM InfoSphere DataStage for Non-Production increased from 10,220 to 11,060 adding $12.5K in deployment costs
ELA renewal deadline of June 30, 2025 requires immediate action on license optimization decisions
IASP ELP delivery to IBM due July 31, 2025 for all sub-capacity products under IASP agreement
market_risks: IBM sub-capacity licensing requires continuous ILMT monitoring to maintain compliance position
Products with 0% utilization need deployment validation to confirm actual usage versus discovery gaps
IBM Cloud Pak and containerized product licensing adds complexity to consumption tracking
additional_insights: IBM Cloud Pak for Business Automation at 36.59% utilization represents $768K optimization opportunity
IBM Storage Suite for IBM Cloud Paks shows 0% utilization on 200 licenses worth $592K
IBM InfoSphere Master Data Management Family at 24.11% utilization offers $807K savings potential
Deployment trends show positive reduction in 10 products including $85K savings from IBM MQ reduction
next_steps: Implement cost savings recommendations by June 30, 2025 target date
Schedule WatsonX implementation with product stakeholders
Begin Non-PVU data collection for annual baseline establishment
Determine license counts for ELA renewal due June 30, 2025
Submit IASP ELP to IBM by July 31, 2025 with Anglepoint copied
